must_detect.matched_text:
Term Sheet — Proposed acquisition of Halcyon Merchant Services Ltd by Wrenbridge Holdings plc (admitted to a UK regulated market), dated 05 June 2026.
Consideration: £118,000,000 cash on completion plus £12,000,000 earn-out over 24 months; financing via committed £60,000,000 bridge from Greyfen Bank Ltd and internal cash.
Conditions: change in control approval under s178 FSMA by the UK competent authority, payment services regulatory consents, no material adverse change, and any required competition clearance.
UK MAR: the parties will maintain insider lists, restrict access to inside information via a clean team, and issue a cleansing announcement on signing; this term sheet does not itself contain inside information.
Confidentiality and DPA: NDA in place; Data Processing Addendum Schedule 2 appended to this term sheet sets out processor instructions and data minimisation for diligence datasets.
Cross-border transfers: Data-Export Approval DE-14 authorises transfer of redacted HR onboarding forms from Halcyon (UK) to CloudShire Analytics Inc. (US) using the UK IDTA Addendum; approval completed on 28 May 2026.
Privacy events completed: DSAR from former employee Mr. Jory Fen was fulfilled and closed on 02 June 2026 with erasure effected except payroll records retained for six years under UK tax law, as logged in Compliance Memo CM-22; no unresolved DSARs remain.
Consent management: Target confirms that Ms. Alia Penfold withdrew marketing consent on 10 May 2026 and suppression is active; no further direct marketing will be undertaken in diligence.
Data minimisation and retention: clean-room datasets are pseudonymised and field-minimised; Onboarding Form OF-7 for key executives excludes bank details at this stage; Retention Policy RRS-UK-5 applies and non-required copies will be deleted within 30 days post-completion or at long-stop if the deal lapses.
Completed controls (non-live): no bulk cross-border exports beyond DE-14 are scheduled prior to completion; prior quarterly privacy audit CAP-11 corrective actions are fully closed and do not constitute a current risk.
Employment and regulatory: offer letters for designated persons (including Dr. Tomas Rooke, CFO-designate) are conditional on pre-approval by the UK competent authority where applicable and no-poach restrictions for 12 months.
Market conduct: all wall-crossings will be recorded, insider lists kept in the UK competent authority template, and any testing of financial models will use anonymised and synthetic data only.
Announcements and listing rules: any announcement will be coordinated with the company’s sponsor, Calder Rowan Partners Ltd, under the UK Listing Rules and DTRs; no announcement will be made until conditions precedent are satisfied or waived.
Governance and warranties: Target gives standard warranties including data protection compliance and completion of DPA Schedule obligations; a breach cured prior to completion will not trigger termination.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

Mr. Jory Fen was fulfilled and closed on 02 June 2026 with erasure effected except payroll records retained for six years under UK tax law, as logged in Compliance Memo CM-22; no unresolved DSARs remain.
Consent management: Target confirms that Ms. Alia Penfold withdrew marketing consent on 10 May 2026 and suppression is active; no further direct marketing will be undertaken in diligence.
Data minimisation and retention: clean-room datasets are pseudonymised and field-minimised; Onboarding Form OF-7 for key executives excludes bank details at this stage; Retention Policy RRS-UK-5 applies and non-required copies will be deleted within 30 days post-completion or at long-stop if the deal lapses.
Completed controls (non-live): no bulk cross-border exports beyond DE-14 are scheduled prior to completion; prior quarterly privacy audit CAP-11 corrective actions are fully closed and do not constitute a current risk.
Employment and regulatory: offer letters for designated persons (including Dr. Tomas Rooke, CFO-designate) are conditional on pre-approval by the UK competent authority where applicable and no-poach restrictions for 12 months.
Market conduct: all wall-crossings will be recorded, insider lists kept in the UK competent authority template, and any testing of financial models will use anonymised and synthetic data only.

Contacts: for privacy matters contact Mr. Alastair Niven (DPO) at privacy@wrenbridgeholdings.co.uk, +44 20 3984 7712; for transaction queries contact Ms. Rania Kestrel at r.kestrel@wrenbridgeholdings.co.uk
